name: Smart Charts — Reading Data and Finding the Middle
whatIsIt: At Class 5 we don't just read graphs — we ANALYSE them. From a bar graph or table we can find the total, the difference, the MOST COMMON value (mode), and even the AVERAGE (add all the values, then share equally by dividing by how many). Smart charts turn data into decisions.
whyNeeded: Reports, sports stats, weather, and surveys are full of charts. Being able to total them, compare them, and find the average or the most common value is how people make sense of information and make fair decisions.
realLifeUses: Average runs a batsman scores across matches
The most common shoe size in a class (for ordering)
Total rainfall over a week from a bar graph
Average marks of a group of friends

vocabulary.meaning: Add all values, then divide by how many there are.
The value that appears most often, or the tallest bar.

story.title: Rohit's Batting Average
story.text: Rohit scored 20, 30, 10 and 40 runs in four matches. 'What's your average?' asks Coach. Rohit adds them: 20 + 30 + 10 + 40 = 100. Then he shares equally over 4 matches: 100 ÷ 4 = 25. 'My average is 25 runs a match,' he says. 'The average smooths the ups and downs into one fair number.'
story.extractedProblem: Scores 20, 30, 10, 40 across 4 matches — what is the average?

visual.caption: Total 100 runs over 4 matches → average 100 ÷ 4 = 25. The average is one fair 'middle' number.

teachingGallery.title: Total, most common, average
teachingGallery.note: Add for total; tallest = most common; total ÷ count = average.

examples.caption: Wed is the most common tally? No — tallest = most (7).

standardMethod.summary: Read the chart, then compute: total (add), most common (tallest bar), or average (total ÷ number of values).
steps: Read each bar/row into a number.
MOST COMMON (mode): the tallest bar / value that appears most.
AVERAGE (mean): add all values, then divide by how many values there are.

workedExamples.problem: Marks of 5 friends: 8, 6, 10, 6, 10. What is their average?
In the same marks (8, 6, 10, 6, 10), what is the most common mark?
steps: Both 6 and 10 appear most (twice) — the data has two modes.

commonMistakes.mistake: Giving the TOTAL when the question asks for the AVERAGE.
commonMistakes.fix: Average = total ÷ number of values. Don't stop at the total — divide by how many.

commonMistakes.mistake: Dividing by the wrong number when finding the average (not the count of values).
commonMistakes.fix: Divide the total by HOW MANY values there are, not by any random number in the data.

easy.q: The average of a set of numbers is…
hintLadder: Add, then divide by the count.

challenge.q: Runs in 4 matches: 15, 25, 30, 10. What is the average?
Ravi adds 20 + 30 + 40 = 90 and says 'the average is 90'. What did he skip?
hintLadder: 90 is the total, not the average.

questions.q: To find the average of 6 values you divide the total by…

teachBackPrompt: Collect a small set of numbers at home (ages of family members, or marks in a game over a few rounds). Draw a quick bar graph, then explain to a grown-up how to find the total, the most common value, and the average.

revisionCard.summary: From a chart: TOTAL = add all values; MOST COMMON (mode) = tallest bar / most frequent; AVERAGE (mean) = total ÷ number of values. Don't stop at the total — divide by HOW MANY to get the average.

meta.curriculum: CBSE/NCERT Class 5 (smart charts — data analysis & average)
